name: Administration Building
description: Burnham's nerve center: papers, telegraphs, mud on boots, tempers under restraint, and the strain of a city trying to stage a modern wonder.
possibleClues: Shipping memorandum with altered crate numbers
A telegraph marked urgent but unsigned
A list of delayed electrical parts

name: Court of Honor
description: Brilliant white facades, open vistas, workers moving through grandeur still half-finished, beauty stretched over exhaustion.
possibleClues: A worker's rumor about after-hours visitors
Fresh wheel marks where none should be

name: Midway Plaisance
description: Noise, spectacle, concessionaires, foreign exhibits, and loose tongues among men who assume no one is listening closely.
possibleClues: A conversation in French cut short at your approach
A receipt tied to a private carriage
A rumor of a meeting at a hotel off the grounds

name: Machinery Hall
description: Steel, heat, engines, belts, and the nervous confidence of modern industry on display.
possibleClues: A maintenance log entry in the wrong hand

name: Freight Yards and Service Access
description: Crates, mud, rail sidings, laborers, and the real arteries beneath the fair's polished face.
possibleClues: A wagon ledger with missing hours

name: Auditorium Hotel Lounge
description: Cigars, polished wood, politics, foreign visitors, and private conversations disguised as idle society chatter.
possibleClues: A meeting arranged under another name
A newspaper draft hinting at scandal
A handwritten note mentioning opening night lighting
